title: Utility Functions for Plotting
text: thisplot R package providing utility functions data visualization plotting. includes tools color manipulation (blending, conversion, palettes), plot customization (themes, grob operations, patchwork building), panel size control, data optimization plots, layout adjustments. Designed enhance workflows ggplot2, patchwork, ComplexHeatmap.

title: Utility Functions for Plotting
text: thisplot provides comprehensive Chinese traditional color system 1058 representative colors. Use ChineseColors() create color object, access colors name (pinyin Chinese), visualize collection visual_colors(), retrieve specific palettes using get_colors(). Example usage:
code: library(thisplot) cc <- ChineseColors() cc #>  #> ── Chinese Traditional Colors System #>  #> ── Total 1058 colors #> • blue: 92 colors #> • cyan: 51 colors #> • gray_brown: 323 colors #> • green: 123 colors #> • orange: 92 colors #> • purple: 95 colors #> • red: 181 colors #> • yellow: 101 colors #>  #> ── Methods: #> • visual_colors(loc_range, num_per_row, title, name_type) #>  #> ── See also: #> • [get_colors()] for searching colors cc$visual_colors(   loc_range = c(1, 180),   num_per_row = 30,   title = "Chinese traditional colors",   name_type = "chinese" ) get_colors("Paired") what: Blend two colors using a specified mode — Blend2Color
title: Blend two colors using a specified mode — Blend2Color
text: Blend two colors alpha channels using one several blending modes: blend, average, screen, multiply.

title: Blend two colors using a specified mode — Blend2Color
code: Blend2Color(C1, C2, mode = "blend")

title: Blend two colors using a specified mode — Blend2Color
text: C1 list containing first color RGB values alpha channel. C2 list containing second color RGB values alpha channel. mode blending mode use. One "blend", "average", "screen", "multiply". Default "blend".

title: Blend two colors using a specified mode — Blend2Color
text: list containing blended RGB values alpha channel.

what: Blend a list of colors — BlendRGBList
title: Blend a list of colors — BlendRGBList
text: Blend multiple colors alpha channels single color using specified blending mode.

title: Blend a list of colors — BlendRGBList
code: BlendRGBList(Clist, mode = "blend", RGB_BackGround = c(1, 1, 1))

title: Blend a list of colors — BlendRGBList
text: Clist list colors, color list containing RGB values alpha channel. mode blending mode use. One "blend", "average", "screen", "multiply". Default "blend". RGB_BackGround background RGB color composite . Default c(1, 1, 1) (white).

title: Blend a list of colors — BlendRGBList
text: numeric vector RGB values.

title: Set the panel width/height of a plot to a fixed value — panel_fix
text: x ggplot object, grob object, combined plot made patchwork cowplot package. panel_index Specify panel fixed. Default 1. padding padding add around panel, inches. Default 0. units units height, width margin given. Can "mm", "cm", "", etc. See grid::unit. raster Whether rasterize panel. dpi Plot resolution. return_grob Whether return grob object instead wrapped patchwork object. title: Set the panel width/height of a plot to a fixed value — panel_fix
text: return_grob TRUE, returns gtable object. Otherwise, returns patchwork object fixed panel sizes. returned object size attribute containing width, height, units.

title: Set the panel width/height of a plot to a fixed value — panel_fix
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(   data = mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt, colour = cyl) ) +   geom_point() +   facet_wrap(~gear, nrow = 2) # fix the size of panel panel_fix(   p,   width = 5,   height = 3,   units = "cm" )  # rasterize the panel panel_fix(   p,   width = 5,   height = 3,   units = "cm",   raster = TRUE,   dpi = 90 )   # `panel_fix` will build and render the plot when input a ggplot object. # so after `panel_fix`, the size of the object will be changed. object.size(p) #> 368016 bytes object.size(   panel_fix(     p,     width = 5,     height = 3,     units = "cm"   ) ) #> 1376144 bytes

title: Shorten and offset the segment — segements_df
code: library(ggplot2) temp_nodes <- data.frame(   "x" = c(10, 40),   "y" = c(10, 30) ) data <- data.frame(   "x" = c(10, 40),   "y" = c(10, 30),   "xend" = c(40, 10),   "yend" = c(30, 10) )  ggplot(temp_nodes, aes(x = x, y = y)) +   geom_point(size = 12) +   xlim(0, 50) +   ylim(0, 50) +   geom_segment(     data = data,     aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend)   )   ggplot(temp_nodes, aes(x = x, y = y)) +   geom_point(size = 12) +   xlim(0, 50) +   ylim(0, 50) +   geom_segment(     data = segements_df(       data,       shorten_start = 2,       shorten_end = 3,       offset = 1     ),     aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend)   )

title: thisplot 0.3.8
text: to_percent(): convert values percentages unit-sum normalization. annotate_quadrants(): add quadrant guide lines percentage labels scatter plots. clip_symmetric_range(): clip values symmetric lower upper limits. jitter_highlighted_points(): deterministically jitter highlighted points stable plotting output. visual_colors(): add adaptive label layout via label_mode, tighter HTML rendering Chinese, pinyin, RGB, hex labels. ChineseColors$visual_colors(): pass name_type layout mode different label types use matching display rules. get_chinese_palettes(): use color_sets$Chinese default Chinese palette add fallback palette definition dataset metadata incomplete. Add runnable examples migration helper functions. Add documentation entries annotate_quadrants(), clip_symmetric_range(), jitter_highlighted_points(), to_percent(). Expand Chinese color examples show default color_sets$Chinese palette. Update visual_colors() documentation new adaptive label layout mode. Refresh Chinese color palette metadata dataset defaults used ChineseColors(). Improve Chinese color ordering default palette selection used generated palette sets.

title: thisplot 0.3.7
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-02 GraphPlot(): New function graph visualization node/edge layers, optional transition arrows, highlighting, labels, palettes, theme customization. compute_velocity_on_grid(): New function compute smoothed velocity vectors embedding grid, optional adjustment stream-style visualization. palette_colors(): Changed default palette "Paired" "Chinese". Refined documentation annotation_block_fill_graphics() build_heatmap_annotation(). Added ggnewscale reshape2 Imports.

title: thisplot 0.3.6
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-07 StatPlot(): Optimized venn plot rendering using coord_fixed(ratio = 1, clip = ""). annotation_block_fill_graphics() annotation_block_graphics() annotation_graphics() build_heatmap_annotation() cluster_within_group2() heatmap_fixsize() heatmap_rendersize() normalize_drawable()

title: thisplot 0.3.5
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-23 make_long(): Converts wide data frame long format geom_sankey / geom_alluvial, optional value column weights. geom_sankey(): Sankey diagram layer (nodes flows). geom_sankey_label(), geom_sankey_text(): Sankey node label layers. geom_alluvial(): Alluvial diagram layer. geom_alluvial_label(), geom_alluvial_text(): Alluvial label layers. geom_sankey_bump(): Sankey bump diagram layer. theme_sankey(), theme_alluvial(), theme_sankey_bump(): Themes sankey/alluvial plots.

title: thisplot 0.3.4
text: StatPlot(): New function visualize data using various plot types including bar plots, rose plots, ring plots, pie charts, trend plots, area plots, dot plots, sankey plots, chord plots, venn diagrams, upset plots. Supports grouping, splitting, background coloring, extensive customization options. StatPlot(): Added comprehensive parameter documentation function arguments including group., split., palette, palcolor, title, subtitle, xlab, ylab, legend.position, legend.direction, theme_use, theme_args, combine, nrow, ncol, byrow, force, seed. StatPlot(): Updated examples use generic test data instead single-cell specific datasets, making function accessible general-purpose plotting tool.

title: thisplot 0.3.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-11 README: Converted static Markdown R Markdown (README.Rmd) dynamic content generation. Code examples now execute display actual output instead static images.

title: thisplot 0.3.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-17 check_ci_env(): New function detect current environment supports browsable HTML output. Now detects local pkgdown builds via IN_PKGDOWN environment variable knitr HTML output contexts. head.colors(): New S3 method colors objects, allowing use head() function limit number displayed rows (default n = 6L). visual_colors(): Integrated htmltools::browsable functionality. Added browsable parameter (default NULL auto-detection) automatically display widgets appropriate environments. Automatically detects interactive sessions GitHub Actions pkgdown workflow, preventing browser opening CRAN checks. ChineseColors$visual_colors(): Added browsable parameter support, consistent global visual_colors() function. get_colors(): Now automatically infers RGB values hex codes colors found palettes main dataset. Added internal hex_to_rgb() helper function convert hex color codes RGB format strings.

title: thisplot 0.3.0
text: get_colors(): New function search colors Chinese colors dataset available palettes. Supports searching palette names, color names (pinyin Chinese), numbers, hex codes. Automatically reports palette(s) contain found colors. print.colors(): New print method colors objects display color information ANSI color support terminal. Updated Chinese color system improved functionality.

title: thisplot 0.2.0
text: ChineseColors(): New function create Chinese traditional color system object 1058 representative colors extracted traditional Chinese color charts. Supports accessing colors name (pinyin Chinese), number, hex code, creating color palettes, generating segmented colormaps. visual_colors(): New function visualize colors HTML format grid color swatches, supporting optional names, RGB values, hex codes. get_chinese_palettes(): New function get Chinese color palettes organized color categories (blue, green, yellow, red, brown, purple, etc.) automatic continuous palette generation. chinese_colors: New data object containing 1058 Chinese traditional colors. Added comprehensive documentation Chinese color system functions. Updated package documentation include Chinese traditional colors feature.
